mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and subdued consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in quarterly OLS regressions or forward returns turn negative when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with Spearman rho below 0.05 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 50% of high-inflation months over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by greater than 2% annually over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal expanding residential investment and broader economic confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows or returns are negative in greater than 55% of months with rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates severe demand-supply mismatch and economic stagnation, triggering portfolio de-risking that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of low-GDP-growth quarters over a 15-year historical sample.
